source_title: Data Visualization Tutorial For Beginners | Big Data Analytics Tutorial | Simplilearn

concepts.name: Data Visualization
concepts.definition: The technique of presenting data in a pictorial or graphical format to identify trends, patterns, and relationships in complex datasets.

concepts.name: Matplotlib
concepts.definition: A multi-platform, 2D python plotting library built on NumPy and SciPy used for creating interactive, high-quality visualizations.

concepts.name: Plot Elements
concepts.definition: Constituent parts of a graph including the Grid (background), Title (description), Legend (labeling of variables), and Axes (coordinates).

steps.explanation: Load Matplotlib for plotting and NumPy for numerical data handling.
steps.code_snippet:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import style
steps.gotcha: Forgetting '%matplotlib inline' in Jupyter will prevent plots from showing automatically.

steps.explanation: Prepare the numeric or categorical values to be visualized.
steps.gotcha: Data arrays must be of equal length for X and Y axes.

steps.explanation: Configure styles, labels, titles, and line properties.
steps.gotcha: Setting parameters after the show() command will not apply them to the current plot.

steps.explanation: Render the graphic to the screen or notebook.
steps.gotcha: In script mode, the code will hang until the plot window is closed.

code_recipes.name: Simple 2D Line Plot with Customization
code_recipes.purpose: To create a styled line graph with custom colors, line styles, and axis limits.
code_recipes.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import style
%matplotlib inline

# Data
web_customers = [123, 645, 950, 1290, 1630, 1450, 1034, 1295, 465]
time_hrs = [7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15]

# Style and Plot
style.use('ggplot')
plt.plot(time_hrs, web_customers, color='b', linestyle='--', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.4)

# Axis and Labels
plt.axis([6.5, 17.5, 50, 2000])
plt.xlabel('Time (hrs)')
plt.ylabel('Users')
plt.title('Website Traffic')

# Annotation
plt.annotate('Max', ha='center', va='bottom', xytext=(11, 1630), xy=(11, 1630))

plt.show()

gotchas.description: Plot not appearing in Jupyter Notebook.
gotchas.context: Executing plot code in a Jupyter cell without specific backend configuration.
gotchas.solution: Include the magic command `%matplotlib inline` at the top of the notebook.

gotchas.context: When using multiple lists of data.
gotchas.solution: Ensure that the input lists (X and Y variables) have identical lengths before calling plt.plot().
